title: Docs
use_when: Use Docs when shipped behavior, product changes, API changes, onboarding flows, migration steps, support guidance, or release details need documentation review for accuracy, discoverability, consistency, and update scope.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Docs for product strategy, product requirements, UX critique, engineering architecture, QA execution, release go/no-go, DX usability testing, or stakeholder comms unless written guidance is the primary risk.
output_artifact: Docs gate report with docs scope, consistency check, update plan, decision, and handoff
framework_fit: Docs fits the documentation gate after product or code changes and before release or support handoff, where written guidance must match shipped behavior and be discoverable by the intended audience.
failure_modes: Updates documentation broadly without tying each change to actual shipped behavior, affected audience, or a verified contradiction.
Treats docs as release notes only and misses README, guides, help content, API docs, migration notes, support content, and cross-document consistency.
Lets release proceed with stale or undiscoverable guidance when docs are required for users, support, developers, or stakeholders to succeed.

examples.prompt: Use $docs to review documentation consistency after this shipped behavior change and identify the exact README, guide, and migration updates needed.
examples.expected_artifact: Docs gate report with docs scope, consistency check, update plan, decision, and handoff
